notes.note: Eques Romanus: a member of the equestrian order, the second of the propertied orders below the senatorial. The status, marked by the gold ring and the public horse, was a standard guarantor of standing in commendation letters.

In meo aere esse: a financial idiom from the account-ledger (aes), used metaphorically. Cicero is saying that he reckons Silius among those whose interests he has formally taken on; the bond is mediated through their mutual friend Lamia.
